titlestmt.author: Compiled by Susan Brady and Arts Library Staff

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday May 20, 2019 at 23:00
Transformed with yale.addEadidUrl.xsl. Adds @url with handle for finding aid. Overwrites @url if already present.

did.unittitle: Volvelle collection
did.abstract: Jessica Helfand, a graduate of the Yale School of Art Graphic Design Program (MFA '89) and a respected critic of visual culture, collected hundreds of examples of the volvelle format. In December of 2004, Helfand donated the collection to the Arts Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has not been transferred to Yale University.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Volvelle Collection, Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library, Yale University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Jessica Helfand, a graduate of the Yale School of Art Graphic Design Program (MFA '89) and a respected critic of visual culture, collected hundreds of examples of the volvelle format. In December of 2004, Helfand donated the collection to the Arts Library. Volvelles, or graphic wheels, are "various reckoning devices consisting of movable discs surmounted by or carrying other graduated or figured circles for calculating, for example, phases of the moon, the time of the rising or setting sun, or the times of the tides."1 They are efficient tools for collating a large amount of information into a compact delivery device, yet have also been used purely for novelty. Topics of the volvelles range from astronomy to pop culture and fashion. Several, such as language and geographic wheels, serve as educational tools; others have more technical and scientific applications. The volvelles are made of a variety of materials including many forms of paper and card stock, plastic, metal, and leather. Helfand's book, Reinventing the Wheel, which was published by Princeton Architectural Press in 2002, served as the catalogue for an exhibition of the same title at The Grolier Club in New York City in the spring of 2004. The volume contains many of the volvelles that are a part of this collection, although some titles vary from those listed in this finding aid. Ms. Helfand also gave a number of books containing volvelles to the Library. The books are catalogued individually in Orbis, Yale's online catalog, and can be found by conducting a Special Collections Subject search for "volvelle collection." In addition to the volvelles, the collection contains one box of Helfand's files relating to her book and to her Grolier Club exhibit.

1
Art and Architecture Thesaurus . 2004. J. Paul Getty Trust. 1 August 2005. <http://www.getty.edu/research/conducting_research/vocabularies/aat/>.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The volvelles are arranged alphabetically by title within various size categories. Each volvelle title has a unique number; pamphlets, brochures, etc. that accompany volvelles are filed under the number of the respective volvelle. A box listing of Helfand's files follows the listing of the volvelles.

did.unittitle: Volvelles – Under 11"
1. Abbotts Fairmont Know Your Presidents. [S.l.]: Abbotts Fairmont, n.d. Wheel.
2. Acme Breeding Calculator. Chicago, IL: J.B. Carroll Co., 1928. Wheel.
3. Advance Spectacle Co., Inc. Hold Close to Eye. Chicago, IL: Advance Spectacle, Co., Inc., n.d. Wheel.
4. Aircraft Spotter Dial. [S.l.]: Wonder Bread, n.d. Wheel.
5. Alfigen/The Genetics Institute Pregnancy Calculator. Pasadena, CA: Alfigen/The Genetics Institute, n.d. Wheel.
6. All-American Services Insignia Encyclopedia. Chicago, IL: King-Larson McMahon, 1943. Wheel.
7. Allen Scope Dial-Check Pattern Conditions. Kalamazoo, MI: Allen Electric and Equipment Company, 1958. Wheel.
8. Ansco Exposure Calculator for Long Bellows Extensions. Binghamton, NY: General Aniline & Film Corporation, 1950. Wheel.
9. Anti-Stain Formulary Central Scientific Company. New York, NY: Central Scientific Company, 1915. Wheel.
10. Armour's Mystic Oracle. Chicago, IL: Armour and Company, n.d. Wheel.
11. Army-Navy Insignia Guide. New York, NY: Advertisers' Serv. Div., 1943. Wheel.
12. Around the Stations of the Cross. New York, NY: Charles R. Isola, 1933. Wheel and brochure.
13. Average Monthly Cost of Operating Large Appliances Ready Kilowatt. [S.l.]: Philadelphia Electric Company, n.d. Wheel.

15. Batter Up [Scorer]. [S.l.]: [S.n.], n.d. Wheel.

19. Boy's Life Additional Enrollment Calculator. [S.l.]: [S.n.], n.d. Wheel.
20. Breeders Guide Information Desired "Seen at a Glance". Peterborough, NH: The American Art Works Inc., n.d. Wheel.

Volvelles – 2008 Addition

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The volvelles are arranged in the original alphabetical order imposed by the collector.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Items #55-58 were not listed on the "Addition July 2008 Box 13" inventory.

40. Positive Turn Life Tools: Marital Harmony Chart 1A and 1B. S.1., Art & Content, 2004. Wheel in packaging.

Folder of Miscellaneous Paper

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder includes newspaper clippings about volvelles; a cover of The New York Times , dated December 17, 2006, featuring a "Donormeter;" a booklet from Perrygraf on "Powerful Tools for Profitable Selling;" a booklet from American Slide Chart on "Branding and Solutions;" an article from The Week , dated January 22-29, 2007; and 21 pages of printouts showing various wheels.

did.unittitle: Volvelles – 2009 Addition 1 inventory
